Title: BpWRKY6 regulates insect resistance by affecting jasmonic acid and terpenoid synthesis in Betula platyphylla

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in BpWRKY6-overexpression lines; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid mediates anti-insect defense in plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gypsy moth feeding; MeJA-related defense context
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Fatty acid precursor pool leading to oxylipin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BpLOX15 promoter used in dual-luciferase assays
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: JA-pathway intermediate leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis associated with increased insect resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional target of BpWRKY6 through promoter W-box binding
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream marker/stacking target with BpWRKY6 for insect-resistant birch breeding
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxygenation of polyunsaturated fatty acids in the early steps of jasmonate biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpLOX15 expression increased in BpWRKY6-overexpressing birch. 2. BpWRKY6 directly bound the BpLOX15 promoter W-box and activated reporter expression. 3. This supports BpLOX15 as a direct JA-biosynthetic target in the BpWRKY6 insect-defense module.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in BpWRKY6-overexpression lines; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid mediates anti-insect defense in plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gypsy moth feeding; MeJA-related defense context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BpAOC4 promoter used in dual-luciferase assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis associated with increased insect resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct transcriptional target of BpWRKY6; ChIP enrichment specifically detected on promoter fragment BpAOC4-p2
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential co-engineering target to strengthen JA-mediated insect resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpAOC4 was upregulated in BpWRKY6-overexpressing birch. 2. BpWRKY6 directly bound and activated the BpAOC4 promoter via W-box elements. 3. BpAOC4 contributes to elevated JA accumulation in the insect-resistance response.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in BpWRKY6-overexpression lines; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid mediates anti-insect defense in plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Gypsy moth feeding; MeJA-related defense context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BpAOS1 promoter used in dual-luciferase assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes jasmonic acid biosynthesis associated with increased insect resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strongest LUC activation among tested JA genes by BpWRKY6
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential high-value co-target with BpWRKY6 due to strong promoter activation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpAOS1 expression increased in BpWRKY6-overexpressing birch. 2. BpWRKY6 directly bound its promoter W-box and strongly activated reporter expression. 3. BpAOS1 is a key direct JA-pathway target in BpWRKY6-mediated insect defense.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR in BpWRKY6-overexpression lines; dual-luciferase assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; metabolite profiling by GC-MS and total terpenoid assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: TMTT and terpenoids contribute to plant insect defense and can attract natural enemies of pests
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in BpWRKY6-overexpression lines, especially in feeding defense context
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native BpCYP82G1 promoter used in dual-luciferase assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TMTT and total terpenoid accumulation after insect feeding, enhancing resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper refers to CYP82G1 as trimethyltridecatetraene/dimethylnonatriene synthase; direct target of BpWRKY6
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising downstream target for engineering volatile-mediated insect resistance in birch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of herbivory-associated homoterpene TMTT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; GC-MS metabolite profiling, accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpCYP82G1 was upregulated in BpWRKY6-overexpressing birch. 2. BpWRKY6 directly bound and activated the BpCYP82G1 promoter via W-box elements. 3. Increased BpCYP82G1 expression correlated with higher TMTT and total terpenoids after gypsy moth feeding.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering insect-resistant birch by enhancing JA and terpenoid defense pathways; may be stacked with downstream biosynthetic genes or MAPK signaling components
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid and terpenoids mediate plant defense against insects; TMTT may attract natural enemies of pests
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation of target promoters via W-box binding; activation enhanced by phosphorylation from BpMAPK6
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for subcellular localization construct; pCAMBIA1300-39Flag overexpression construct promoter not clearly specified; native target promoters used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and CRISPR mutant analysis in birch; insect feeding assays; JA/TMTT/total terpenoid quantification; qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; pull-down; in vitro phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY transcription factor, subgroup IIa, WRKY domain with C2H2 zinc finger
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Induced by MeJA, suggesting positive/feedforward involvement with JA signaling; explicit feedback loop not fully established
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gypsy moth feeding; MeJA treatment; BpMAPK6-mediated phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates BpLOX15, BpAOC4, BpAOS1, and BpCYP82G1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpWRKY6 is a nucleus-localized WRKY IIa transcription factor induced by gypsy moth feeding and MeJA. 2. Overexpression increased birch resistance to Lymantria dispar, elevated JA, TMTT, and total terpenoids, and delayed larval development, while mutant lines showed the opposite trend for JA and some defense traits. 3. BpWRKY6 directly bound W-box motifs in BpLOX15, BpAOC4, BpAOS1, and BpCYP82G1 promoters to activate their expression. 4. BpMAPK6 interacted with and phosphorylated BpWRKY6, enhancing downstream target activation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BpWRKY6 lacks intrinsic transcriptional activation activity in yeast; did not directly activate BpAOS2 despite promoter W-boxes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates JA and terpenoid synthesis, thereby increasing insect resistance in birch
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of jasmonic acid and volatile terpenoids in herbivore defense
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly induced in leaves after gypsy moth feeding, especially at low feeding damage; induced by MeJA up to 24 h
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with BpMAPK6; likely functions in a phosphorylation-dependent regulatory module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaves; induced by herbivory and MeJA
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of overexpression lines, accession NA; JA by UPLC-MS/MS; TMTT by GC-MS
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct upstream regulator linking herbivory/MAPK signaling to JA and terpenoid accumulation; decisive positive controller of insect-defense metabolite production

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BpWRKY6 protein; indirectly BpLOX15; BpAOC4; BpAOS1; BpCYP82G1 via BpWRKY6
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential signaling-engineering target to potentiate BpWRKY6-mediated insect resistance, though broader MAPK pleiotropy should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid and terpenoids support plant anti-insect defense
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction and phosphorylation-mediated activation of BpWRKY6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; pull-down; in vitro phosphorylation; phosphosite mutation analysis; dual-luciferase enhancement assay
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gypsy moth feeding signal; MAPK signaling pathway context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enhances BpWRKY6-mediated activation of BpLOX15, BpAOC4, BpAOS1, and BpCYP82G1 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BpMAPK6 physically interacted with BpWRKY6. 2. BpMAPK6 phosphorylated BpWRKY6 at Ser253 and Ser288 in vitro. 3. Coexpression of BpMAPK6 enhanced BpWRKY6 activation of downstream JA and terpenoid biosynthetic gene promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of metabolite accumulation through a signaling role rather than direct transcriptional control
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances BpWRKY6 function and thereby promotes JA/terpenoid-defense gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Signal transduction controlling defense metabolite biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest expression when leaf area consumed by gypsy moth larvae was less than one quarter
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms an interaction pair with BpWRKY6
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in leaves after gypsy moth feeding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent upstream enhancer of the core BpWRKY6 defense regulatory module
